zh.lifeSummary: 當Joan de Beauchamp於1365年出生在英國諾薩普頓史爾時，她的父親John De Beauchamp41歲，母親Joan Bridport31歲。她於1378年與Thomas John Bedlisgate結婚。她去世於1394年9月4日在英國英格蘭越可爾葛拉芙頓享年29歲，埋葬在英國肯特麥德斯同。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Joan de Beauchamp于1365年出生在英国諾薩普頓史爾时，她的父亲John De Beauchamp41岁，母亲Joan Bridport31岁。她于1378年与Thomas John Bedlisgate结婚。她去世于1394年9月4日在英国英格兰越可爾葛拉芙頓享年29岁，埋葬在英国肯特麥德斯同。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Beauchamp
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Antoine, Jacques, Yves, Andre, Armand, Emile, Fernand, Marcel, Marthe, Remi, Alcide, Camille.

English (of Norman origin) and French: habitational name from any of several places in France, for example in Manche and Somme, that are named with Old French beu, bel ‘fair, lovely’ + champ(s) ‘field, plain’ (from Latin bello campo ‘beautiful field’). In English the surname is generally pronounced Beecham . Compare Bushaw .
English: in some cases, an Anglo-Norman French adaptation of Scottish Campbell , by translation into French of the folk etymology de campo bello ‘from the fair field’.

variants.name: Beecham
meanings: English (Lincolnshire): variant of   Beauchamp  , reflecting the normal English pronunciation.

variants.name: Beachem
meanings: English (Somerset and Gloucestershire): variant of   Beauchamp  .

variants.name: Beachum
meanings: Altered form of English   Beauchamp  .

variants.name: Bagent
meanings: English:
possibly a colloquial pronunciation of Beauchamp or Beecham . Old French Beau- often appears as Be(e)- in English surnames. The -ch- has been voiced and excrescent -t has been added.

perhaps an Anglicized form of a French or Flemish surname such as Beaujean and Bejean, from Old French bel Jehan ‘handsome John’ + excrescent -t. It could have been introduced to England in the 15th or 16th century by Flemish migrants.

variants.name: Beacham
meanings: English (of Norman origin): variant of   Beauchamp  .

variants.name: Campbell
meanings: Scottish: nickname from Gaelic cam ‘crooked, bent’ + beul ‘mouth’. As a result of folk etymology, the surname was often represented in Latin documents as de bello campo ‘of the fair field’, which led to the name sometimes being ‘translated’ into Anglo-Norman French as Beauchamp .
Irish (North Armagh): adopted for Gaelic Mac Cathmhaoil ‘son of Cathmhaol’ (literally ‘battle chief’): see Caulfield and Cowell .
English: variant of   Camel  , under the influence of the Scottish name (see 1 above).

variants.name: Bushaw
meanings: Americanized form of French   Beauchamp   and   Bouchard  .

variants.name: Beacom
meanings: English (Somerset) and Irish (Fermanagh): of Norman origin, a variant of   Beauchamp  .

nameMeanings.name: Joan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

variants.name: John
meanings: As a boy's name this represents a revival of Middle English Jan, a by‐form of John , or an adoption of the common European form with this spelling. As a girl's name it is a short form of names such as Janet and Janice .

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Joan de Beauchamp was born in 1365, in Northamptonshire, England, her father, John De Beauchamp, was 41 and her mother, Joan Bridport, was 31. She married Thomas John Bedlisgate in 1378. She died on 4 September 1394, in Grafton, Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 29, and was buried in Maidstone, Kent, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Joan de Beauchamp est née en 1365, à Northamptonshire, Angleterre, son père, John De Beauchamp, avait 41 ans et sa mère, Joan Bridport, avait 31 ans. Elle a épousé Thomas John Bedlisgate en 1378. Elle est décédée le 4 septembre 1394, à Grafton, Everwyck, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 29 ans, et a été enterrée à Maidstone, Kent, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Joan de Beauchamp 1365 in Northamptonshire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater John De Beauchamp 41 und ihre Mutter Joan Bridport 31. Sie hat 1378 Thomas John Bedlisgate geheiratet. Sie starb am 4. September 1394 im Alter von 29 Jahren in Grafton, Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom und wurde in Maidstone, Kent, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Joan de Beauchamp è nata il nel 1365 nel Northamptonshire, England, sua padre, John De Beauchamp, aveva 41 anni e sua madre, Joan Bridport, aveva 31 anni. Lei sposato Thomas John Bedlisgate nel 1378. Morì il 4 settembre 1394, nel Grafton, Yorkshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 29 anni, e fu sepolta a Maidstone, Kent, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Joan de Beauchampが1365年にイギリスノーサンプトンシュアイアで生まれた時，父John De Beauchampは41歳，母Joan Bridportは31歳であった。1378年にThomas John Bedlisgateと結婚した。彼女は1394年9月4日にイギリスイギリスヨークシュアイアグラフトンで29歳で死去し，イギリスケントメイドストーンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Joan de Beauchamp(이)가 1365년 영국 노르섬프토실르에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 John De Beauchamp(은)는 41세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Joan Bridport(은)는 31세였다. 그녀는 1378년 Thomas John Bedlisgate(와)과 결혼했다. 그녀는 1394년 9월 4일 영국 잉글랜드 요르크실르 그라프턴에서 29세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 켄트 메이드스톤에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Joan de Beauchamp nasceu em 1365, em Northamptonshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, John De Beauchamp, tinha 41 anos e sua mãe, Joan Bridport, tinha 31 anos. Ela casou-se com Thomas John Bedlisgate em 1378. Ela faleceu em 4 de setembro de 1394, em Grafton, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 29 anos, e foi sepultada em Maidstone, Kent, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Joan de Beauchamp родилась в 1365 г. в местности нортамптоншир, Англия, её отцу по имени John De Beauchamp было 41 год, а матери, которую звали Joan Bridport, 31. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Thomas John Bedlisgate в 1378 г. Она умерла 4 сентября 1394 г. в местности графтон, еркшир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 29 лет и была похоронена в местности меидстоун, кэнт, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Joan de Beauchamp nació en 1365, en Northamptonshire, Inglaterra, su papá, John De Beauchamp, tenía 41 años y su mamá, Joan Bridport, tenía 31 años. Se casó con Thomas John Bedlisgate en 1378. Murió el 4 de septiembre de 1394, en Grafton, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 29 años, y fue sepultada en Maidstone, Kent, Inglaterra.
